context.title: Sustainability reporting project recovery
context.description: The workstream lost momentum after conflicting stakeholder feedback and a missed internal checkpoint.

trigger.summary: Conflicting feedback changed the expected deliverable after the checkpoint was missed.

impact.description: The publication timeline moved and ownership became unclear.

actions.title: Break the work into a 48-hour recovery task

response.current_strategy: Reduce scope to the decision needed for the next checkpoint.

assumptions: The final decision owner was understood by everyone.
adaptations: Record the decision owner before the next review cycle.

next_steps.success_signal: One decision owner, one approved scope, and a dated next checkpoint.

missing_context.prompt: List the work, decisions, or relationships affected.

confidence.rationale: Confidence reflects recorded context completeness and unresolved contradictions, not certainty about future outcomes.

score_display_policy.message: The composite conditions score must be shown with component explanations and condition maps.

impact_buffer.explanation: Lower recorded impact leaves more near-term recovery room; severity remains visible separately.

pressure_buffer.explanation: Lower recorded pressure increases the room available for deliberate recovery action.

energy_capacity.explanation: Recorded energy is treated as available capacity, not motivation or character.

support_capacity.explanation: Recorded access to support increases recovery capacity.

clarity_capacity.explanation: Recorded clarity supports prioritization and a bounded next step.

action_readiness.explanation: Readiness increases with up to four distinct response or next-step actions.

constraint_manageability.explanation: Manageability reflects the recorded controllability of constraints; no listed constraints defaults to full manageability.

flags.message: Clarify what can pause, wait, or be delegated.
flags.rationale: Pressure is recorded at 8 or above.

recommended_actions.title: Document unresolved assumptions
recommended_actions.rationale: Preserved from the recorded response or next-step plan.

recommended_actions.title: Reduce the work to one near-term checkpoint instead of a full reset.

72_hours.title: Break the work into a 48-hour recovery task

checkpoint.success_signal: One decision owner, one approved scope, and a dated next checkpoint.

plan_rules: At least one action has a named owner.
Blocked and past-target actions are support signals, not performance judgments.
Reassessment creates a new record revision and preserves prior findings.

findings.decision_note: Recorded recovery conditions are assessed as fragile recovery conditions. The composite conditions score is 47.8/100 and must be interpreted with the pressure, constraint, support, capacity, and component maps. Protect available capacity, address the highest-friction condition, and update the record at the next checkpoint.
interpretation_limits: Describes recorded recovery conditions, not a person's character.
Does not diagnose mental health, predict outcomes, or replace professional support.
Must not be used for employee ranking, automated eligibility, or performance evaluation.
Generated findings require human review when used for consequential decisions.

flags.message: Reduce scope and protect recovery time.

flags.message: Avoid overloading the next action plan.
flags.rationale: Energy capacity is recorded at 3 or below.

flags.message: Identify one concrete support channel before expanding work.

flags.message: Define the decision, owner, and next checkpoint.
flags.rationale: Clarity is recorded at 3 or below.

flags.message: Choose a recovery action that can be completed quickly.
flags.rationale: The time horizon is three days or less.

flags.message: Separate adaptation work from constraints requiring accommodation or escalation.
flags.rationale: Two or more constraints are outside direct control.
